task_completion.description: Create a NOTUNIQUE index on Hotels.City
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a database console, GUI, or script output where a NOTUNIQUE index on the 'City' property of the 'Hotels' class is created or listed.

task_completion.description: Create a NOTUNIQUE COMPOSITE index on Hotels covering Country and Stars
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the creation or existence of a NOTUNIQUE composite index on the 'Hotels' class for properties 'Country' and 'Stars' (in that specific order).

task_completion.description: Create a NOTUNIQUE index on Profiles.Nationality
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the creation or existence of a NOTUNIQUE index on the 'Nationality' property of the 'Profiles' class.
A text editor or document showing the EXPLAIN output for all three target queries before the indexes were created (typically showing full class scans or no utilized indexes).
A text editor or document showing the EXPLAIN output for all three target queries after the indexes were created, demonstrating that the queries now utilize the newly created indexes.
A text editor title bar, terminal command (e.g., ls or cat), or file manager showing the report is saved exactly at /home/ga/query_optimization_report.txt.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual database to execute queries and create indexes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a real database client (like OrientDB Studio or console) being used to run the EXPLAIN commands and CREATE INDEX statements.

integrity.description: EXPLAIN outputs in the report appear genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The EXPLAIN results pasted into the report should look like authentic database output (e.g., JSON format or structured text with execution plans), not manually typed or hallucinated summaries.

integrity.description: Query results or execution stats match the actual database content
integrity.visual_evidence: If record counts are visible in the EXPLAIN output, they should match the real data: 1 hotel in Rome, 3 Italian hotels with >= 4 stars, and 3 British profiles.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database contains synthetic travel agency data. Expected query results: `SELECT FROM Hotels WHERE City = 'Rome'` returns 1 record; `SELECT FROM Hotels WHERE Country = 'Italy' AND Stars >= 4` returns 3 records; `SELECT FROM Profiles WHERE Nationality = 'British'` returns 3 records. The required indexes are: NOTUNIQUE on Hotels.City, NOTUNIQUE COMPOSITE on Hotels(Country, Stars), and NOTUNIQUE on Profiles.Nationality. The final report must be saved at /home/ga/query_optimization_report.txt.
